userStories.title: Project setup and layout skeleton
userStories.description: Initialize Next.js 14 project with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and install all required dependencies: react-globe.gl (or three), recharts, framer-motion. Configure Tailwind with dark theme defaults (black/dark navy). Create the root layout with a black background, monospace font for data elements, and a subtle CSS grid background pattern. Set up the main page with three zones: top bar, center globe+sidebar, bottom stats.
acceptanceCriteria: Next.js 14 app with TypeScript compiles without errors
Tailwind configured with custom colors: neon green (#00ff41), neon red (#ff2d2d), dark navy (#0a0e1a)
Global layout renders with black background and grid pattern
Three layout zones visible: top bar, main content area, stats section
All dependencies installed: react-globe.gl, recharts, framer-motion

userStories.title: Mock data layer for all threat zones
userStories.description: Create a TypeScript data file with realistic mock data for all 12 active conflict zones: Ukraine, Gaza, Red Sea, South China Sea, Taiwan Strait, Sahel, Sudan, Myanmar, Syria, Somalia, Yemen, Korean Peninsula. Each zone has: id, name, lat, lng, severity (critical/high/medium/low), region, threat types, involved parties, estimated casualties, duration, NATO response status, and a mini timeline array of 3-5 recent events.
acceptanceCriteria: File src/data/threats.ts exports a typed array of 12 threat zones
Each zone has all required fields: id, name, lat, lng, severity, region, threatTypes, involvedParties, casualties, duration, natoStatus, timeline
Severity values are one of: critical, high, medium, low
Region values cover: Europe, Middle East, Asia-Pacific, Africa
At least 3 threat types represented: armed-conflict, naval, cyber, aerospace, nuclear

userStories.title: Mock data for threat feed alerts and military assets
userStories.description: Create mock data for: (a) threat feed alerts — 20+ scrolling alerts each with timestamp, location, severity, and short description; (b) military assets — ~15 NATO base locations (lat/lng, name, country) and ~5 carrier strike group positions (lat/lng, name, vessel). Export all from src/data/.
acceptanceCriteria: src/data/alerts.ts exports 20+ typed alert objects with timestamp, location, severity, description
src/data/military.ts exports natoBase and carrierGroup arrays with lat/lng and labels
All data is TypeScript typed with exported interfaces
Alerts span multiple regions and severity levels

userStories.title: 3D globe component base render
userStories.description: Build a GlobeComponent using react-globe.gl (or Three.js fallback). Render a dark-themed Earth with glowing country borders (neon green, low opacity). The globe should fill its container, auto-rotate slowly, and support mouse drag to rotate and scroll to zoom. No markers yet — just the globe itself styled to match the dark military aesthetic.
acceptanceCriteria: Globe renders inside a dark container without errors
Country borders visible in neon green or dim green color
Auto-rotation works on load
Mouse drag rotates the globe
Scroll wheel zooms in/out
Globe background is black/dark space, not white

userStories.title: Threat zone markers on globe
userStories.description: Add the 12 threat zone markers to the globe as pulsing circles. Critical = red, high = orange, medium = yellow, low = green. Use CSS animation or Three.js animation to create a pulsing ring effect for each marker. Marker size scales with severity. Markers are visible above the globe surface.
acceptanceCriteria: All 12 threat zones render as colored circles on the globe at correct lat/lng
Color matches severity: critical=red, high=orange, medium=yellow, low=green
Visible pulsing/ripple animation on each marker
Marker size reflects severity (critical largest)
Markers are positioned correctly on the globe surface

userStories.title: Clickable globe markers and state management
userStories.description: Make threat zone markers clickable on the globe. When clicked, set selectedThreat state in the parent page. Use React context or prop drilling to share selectedThreat across the app. Clicking the same marker again or pressing Escape deselects it. The globe should briefly highlight (glow brighter) the clicked marker.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking a globe marker updates selectedThreat state
Selected marker visually highlights (brighter/larger)
Clicking same marker or Escape clears selection
selectedThreat is accessible from the detail card component
No TypeScript errors in event handlers

userStories.title: Top bar with DEFCON indicator and global stats
userStories.description: Build the TopBar component: DEFCON-style 1-5 indicator (display DEFCON 3 from mock data) with color-coded blocks (1=red, 2=orange, 3=yellow, 4=green, 5=blue), total active threats count badge, last updated timestamp that refreshes every 30 seconds. Use glassmorphism panel style (dark semi-transparent background, subtle border). Monospace font for all data values.
acceptanceCriteria: TopBar renders at the top of the page
DEFCON indicator shows 5 blocks, current level highlighted, others dimmed
DEFCON 3 shows yellow as the active block
Active threats count displays correctly from mock data
Last updated timestamp updates every 30 seconds
Glassmorphism panel styling applied

userStories.title: Threat feed side panel (terminal style)
userStories.description: Build the ThreatFeed sidebar panel. Displays a scrolling list of alerts from mock data. Each alert shows: timestamp in HH:MM:SS, location name, severity badge (color-coded), and description text. Styled as a military terminal: black background, neon green monospace text, blinking cursor effect at the top. Panel auto-scrolls to newest alerts. New alerts appear at the top with a flash animation.
acceptanceCriteria: ThreatFeed panel renders to the right of the globe
All 20+ mock alerts are listed
Each alert shows timestamp, location, severity badge, description
Black background with green monospace text styling
Severity badges are color-coded (red/orange/yellow/green)
Blinking cursor element present at panel top
Panel is scrollable

userStories.title: Filter controls bar
userStories.description: Build a FilterBar component below the top bar. Contains three filter groups: Region (All, Europe, Middle East, Asia-Pacific, Africa, Americas), Severity (All, Critical, High, Medium, Low), Threat Type (All, Armed Conflict, Naval, Cyber, Aerospace, Nuclear). Filters are toggleable pill/button style with neon green active state. Filtering updates visible markers on the globe and alerts in the feed panel simultaneously.
acceptanceCriteria: FilterBar renders with all three filter groups
Active filter highlighted in neon green
Selecting a region hides globe markers not in that region
Selecting a severity hides globe markers below that level
Selecting a threat type filters to matching zones only
Threat feed panel also filters to match active filters
All filters can be combined (AND logic)

userStories.title: Threat detail card modal
userStories.description: Build ThreatDetailCard component that appears when a globe marker is clicked. Shows: zone name header, severity badge, threat summary paragraph, involved parties list, estimated casualties, duration active, NATO response status, and a vertical mini timeline (3-5 events with dates). Styled as a glassmorphism panel that slides in from the right or overlays the globe. Has an X close button.
acceptanceCriteria: Detail card renders when selectedThreat is set
Card disappears when closed or Escape pressed
Displays: name, severity, summary, involved parties, casualties, duration, NATO status
Timeline shows 3-5 events in chronological order
Glassmorphism styling with neon accent borders
Close button functional
Card does not overlap the top bar

userStories.title: Stats dashboard — bar chart and donut chart
userStories.description: Build the StatsPanel component in the bottom section. Use Recharts to render: (1) a bar chart of threat count by region with dark theme (black background, neon green bars, white axis labels), (2) a donut chart showing severity distribution (critical/high/medium/low) with matching severity colors. Both charts derive data from the mock threats array.
acceptanceCriteria: StatsPanel renders below the globe+sidebar section
Bar chart shows threat count for each region
Bar chart has dark theme styling (no white background)
Donut chart shows severity distribution with correct colors
Charts use data derived from the mock threats data
No Recharts TypeScript errors

userStories.title: Stats dashboard — trend line and top 5 ranking
userStories.description: Add to StatsPanel: (3) a 30-day trend line chart (Recharts LineChart) using mock daily threat count data showing an upward trend, styled with neon green line on dark background; (4) a top 5 most critical zones ranking list — sorted by severity then by casualty count, displayed as numbered rows with severity color indicator, zone name, and a mini severity bar.
acceptanceCriteria: 30-day trend line renders with mock data (30 data points)
Line chart has dark theme, neon green line
Top 5 ranking list shows 5 zones in order
Each ranking row has: rank number, severity color dot, zone name, severity bar
Ranking is sorted correctly (critical first, then by casualties)

userStories.title: Military assets overlay toggle
userStories.description: Add a toggle button to show/hide military assets on the globe. When enabled: render NATO base locations as blue dots with hover tooltips showing base name and country; render carrier strike group positions as a ship/diamond icon in cyan. Toggle state is stored in React state. Button is in the top bar or filter bar area, clearly labeled 'NATO Assets'.
acceptanceCriteria: Toggle button labeled 'NATO Assets' visible in UI
Clicking toggle shows/hides military asset markers on globe
NATO bases appear as blue dots at correct coordinates
Carrier groups appear as distinct icons (different from base dots)
Hovering a base/carrier shows name label
Toggle button shows active state when overlay is on

userStories.title: Framer Motion animations and polish
userStories.description: Add Framer Motion animations throughout: (1) detail card slides in from right on open, fades out on close; (2) threat feed alerts animate in with a fade+slide when new ones appear; (3) top bar stats numbers count up on first render; (4) filter pills have a spring scale animation on click; (5) stats panel charts fade in on scroll into view. Ensure no animation causes layout shift.
acceptanceCriteria: Detail card has slide-in/fade-out animation
Threat feed items animate in smoothly
DEFCON/stats numbers animate on mount
Filter pill clicks have tactile spring animation
Stats charts animate into view
No layout shifts or jank during animations
Animations respect prefers-reduced-motion
